revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 御嶽神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=三郷市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[三郷市]][[新和 (三郷市)|新和]]4-617 |緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 48 |緯度秒 = 34.5 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 53 |経度秒 = 03.3 |地図 = |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]、[[国之常立神|国常立尊]]、[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 横堀御嶽神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''御嶽神社'''（みたけじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[三郷市]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[元禄]]年間（[[1688年]] - [[1704年]]）に「水防稲荷」と呼ばれる神社を合祀したという記録があることから、少なくとも[[江戸時代]]前期には既に存在していたものと推測される。「東福寺」が[[別当寺]]であった（明治初期に廃寺）<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年、562-563p</ref>。
[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられ、[[1909年]]（明治42年）の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の2社が合祀された。その後、[[江戸川]]の改修工事に伴い[[昭和期]]に2度移転して、現在地に至っている<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]新和仲橋停留所より徒歩2分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年

[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられ、[[1909年]]（明治42年）の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の2社が合祀された。その後、[[江戸川]]の改修工事に伴い[[昭和期]]に2度移転して、現在地に至っている<ref name="神社"></ref>。

{{Shrine | Name = Ontake Shrine | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Misato, Saitama}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|48|34.5|N|139|53|3.3|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Misato, Saitama|ja|三郷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|35|48|34.5|N|139|53|3.3|E|region:JP-11|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Kunino Tokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Status = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Other Names = Yokobori Ontake Shrine | Festivals = | Annual Events = }}
'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Misato, Saitama|ja|三郷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1688–1704), a shrine called **"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)** was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that **Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period**<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年、562-563p</ref>.
The **shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple** **"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),** which acted as its **bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).** However, with the **Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished** in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
In **1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)** under the **modern Shinto shrine ranking system** (近代社格制度). Later, in **1909 (Meiji 42),** as part of **Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine**<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
Due to **the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects**, the shrine **was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)**, eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
== Access == * **2-minute walk** from the **Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop**.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年.

Later, in **1909 (Meiji 42),** as part of **Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine**<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
Due to **the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects**, the shrine **was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)**, eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年、562-563p</ref>.
The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
== Access == * '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Ontake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Misato, Saitama}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|48|34.5|N|139|53|3.3|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Misato, Saitama|ja|三郷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|34.5|N|139|53|3.3|E|region:JP-11|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Kunino Tokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Status = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Other Names = Yokobori Ontake Shrine | Festivals = | Annual Events = }}
'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Misato, Saitama|ja|三郷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年、562-563p</ref>.
The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
== Access == * '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年.

{{Shrine | Name = Ontake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Misato, Saitama}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|48|34.5|N|139|53|3.3|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Misato, Saitama|ja|三郷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|34.5|N|139|53|3.3|E|region:JP-11|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Kunino Tokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Status = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Other Names = Yokobori Ontake Shrine | Festivals = | Annual Events = }}
'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Misato, Saitama|ja|三郷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年、562-563p</ref>.
The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
== Access == * '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年.

{{Shrine | Name = Ontake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Misato, Saitama}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|48|34.5|N|139|53|3.3|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Misato, Saitama|ja|三郷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|34.5|N|139|53|3.3|E|region:JP-11|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}, {{ill|Kunino Tokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Status = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Other Names = Yokobori Ontake Shrine | Festivals = | Annual Events = }}
'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Misato, Saitama|ja|三郷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年、562-563p</ref>.
The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
== Access == * '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年.

'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Misato, Saitama|ja|三郷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年、562-563p</ref>.
The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

{{Shrine | Name = Ontake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Misato, Saitama}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|48|34.5|N|139|53|3.3|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Misato, Saitama|ja|三郷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|34.5|N|139|53|3.3|E|region:JP-11|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Kunino Tokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Status = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Other Names = Yokobori Ontake Shrine | Festivals = | Annual Events = }}
'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Misato, Saitama|ja|三郷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年、562-563p</ref>.
The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
== Access == * '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年.

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Ontake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Misato, Saitama}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|48|34.5|N|139|53|3.3|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Misato, Saitama|ja|三郷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|48|34.5|N|139|53|3.3|E|region:JP-11|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Kunino Tokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命}} | Status = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} | Established = Unknown | Other Names = Yokobori Ontake Shrine | Festivals = | Annual Events = }}
'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Misato, Saitama|ja|三郷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, Japan.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the {{ill|Genroku era|ja|元禄}} (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year 、562-563p</ref>.
The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
== Access == * '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year .

This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year 、562-563p</ref>.

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 御嶽神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=三郷市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|48|34.5|N|139|53|3.3|E}}}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[三郷市]][[新和 (三郷市)|新和]]4-617 |緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 48 |緯度秒 = 34.5 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 53 |経度秒 = 03.3 |地図 = |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]、[[国之常立神|国常立尊]]、[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]] |社格 = 旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 横堀御嶽神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''御嶽神社'''（みたけじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[三郷市]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == 創建年代は不明である。ただ[[元禄]]年間（[[1688年]] - [[1704年]]）に「水防稲荷」と呼ばれる神社を合祀したという記録があることから、少なくとも[[江戸時代]]前期には既に存在していたものと推測される。「東福寺」が[[別当寺]]であった（明治初期に廃寺）<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年、562-563p</ref>。
[[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）、[[近代社格制度]]に基づく「村社」に列せられ、[[1909年]]（明治42年）の[[神社合祀]]により、周辺の2社が合祀された。その後、[[江戸川]]の改修工事に伴い[[昭和期]]に2度移転して、現在地に至っている<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]新和仲橋停留所より徒歩2分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992年

'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Misato, Saitama (city)|Misato, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year 、562-563p</ref>.

The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine Ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

== Access ==
* '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.

'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Misato, Saitama (city)|Misato, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year 、562-563p</ref>.

The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine Ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

== Access ==
* '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.

'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Misato, Saitama (city)|Misato, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year 、562-563p</ref>.

The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine Ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

== Access ==
* '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year .

'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Misato, Saitama (city)|Misato, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year 、562-563p</ref>.

The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine Ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

== Access ==
* '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year .

'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Misato, Saitama (city)|Misato, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year 、562-563p</ref>.

The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine Ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

* '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.

'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Misato, Saitama (city)|Misato, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year 、562-563p</ref>.

The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine Ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

== Access ==
* '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.

'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Misato, Saitama (city)|Misato, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], Japan.

== History ==
The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year 、562-563p</ref>.

The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine Ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

== Access ==
* '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.

Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
== Access == * '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year .

'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Misato, Saitama (city)|Misato, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], Japan.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku era]] (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year 、562-563p</ref>.
The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine Ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
== Access == * '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year .

In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine Ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
== Access == * '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year .

'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Misato, Saitama (city)|en|Misato, Saitama (city)|WD=Q853061|lt=Misato, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, Japan.
== History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku era}} (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year 、562-563p</ref>.
The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine Ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.
== Access == * '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.

== Further Reading == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year .

'''Ontake Shrine''' (御嶽神社, Mitake Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Misato, Saitama (city)|en|Misato, Saitama (city)|WD=Q853061|lt=Misato, Saitama|1=Misato, Saitama (city)}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, Japan.

== History ==
The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. However, records indicate that during the {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku era|1=Genroku}} (1688–1704), a shrine called '''"Suibō Inari" (Water Protection Inari, 水防稲荷)''' was enshrined within its grounds. This suggests that '''Ontake Shrine was already in existence during the early Edo period'''<ref name="shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year 、562-563p</ref>.

The '''shrine was originally associated with the Buddhist temple''' '''"Tōfuku-ji" (東福寺),''' which acted as its '''bettō-ji (別当寺, shrine-adjacent temple).''' However, with the '''Meiji period's policy of separating Buddhism and Shinto (神仏分離, Shinbutsu Bunri), Tōfuku-ji was abolished''' in the early Meiji period<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

In '''1872 (Meiji 5), Ontake Shrine was designated as a "Village Shrine" (村社, sonsha)''' under the '''modern Shinto shrine Ranking system''' (近代社格制度). Later, in '''1909 (Meiji 42),''' as part of '''Japan’s nationwide shrine consolidation policy (神社合祀, Jinja Gōshi), two nearby shrines were merged into Ontake Shrine'''<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

Due to '''the Edo River (江戸川) flood control projects''', the shrine '''was relocated twice during the Shōwa period (昭和期)''', eventually settling at its current location<ref name="shrine"></ref>.

== Access ==
* '''2-minute walk''' from the '''Shinwa Nakahashi (新和仲橋) bus stop'''.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year .
